Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health Simple This scenario outlines a targeted phishing campaign aimed at healthcare professionals, leading to ransomware deployment disrupting critical services.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Email Systems, File System
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Delivery Method, Phishing, Data Encryption

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 467; Name: Cross Site Identification
stage.description: The attackers send spearphishing emails containing malicious links to healthcare professionals, masquerading as trusted entities to gain initial access.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 88; Name: OS Command Injection
stage.description: Once clicked, the link leads to a webpage that executes a Bash script, leveraging legitimate tools for malicious purposes such as executing unauthorized commands.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 609; Name: Cellular Traffic Intercept
stage.description: The executed script deploys ransomware, encrypting files on the system and demanding a ransom for decryption keys, thereby impacting the availability of critical services.

Scenario.summary: APT28 Nearest Neighbor Campaign employs sophisticated spearphishing techniques targeting healthcare professionals, leading to ransomware deployment through execution of malicious scripts, ultimately causing service disruption.
